name: Garden DevOps Automation API
what_it_does: DevOps automation platform with CLI and REST API for orchestrating development, testing, and deployment of microservices — providing smart caching, parallel test execution, and unified workflows for Kubernetes, Docker Compose, and Terraform.
use_cases: Triggering development environment builds and tests programmatically via REST API
Querying test results and build status across microservice graphs for CI agents
Managing ephemeral Kubernetes environments for PR-based testing automation
Deploying microservice applications with dependency-aware ordering via API
Running integration tests across multiple services with automatic service discovery
not_for: Simple single-service applications without microservice complexity
Teams without Kubernetes knowledge — Garden has a learning curve
Pure CI/CD without local development integration needs
best_when: An agent orchestrates complex microservice development and testing workflows where smart caching and dependency-aware builds significantly reduce CI time.
avoid_when: You have simple monolithic apps or small service counts where the overhead of Garden isn't justified.

auth.notes: Garden Cloud uses API token for management. Self-hosted Garden has no API auth (local only). CLI commands can be called programmatically. Team tokens for CI integration.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source core: completely free; Garden Cloud: free for small teams
pricing.paid_tiers: Garden Cloud Team from $500/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Open source core is Apache 2.0. Garden Cloud adds remote caching, team management, and cloud execution. Strong open-source community with enterprise add-on.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no limits; Cloud: rate limits per plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CLI documentation comprehensive. REST API less documented than CLI. Garden Cloud API available for management operations. Error responses include actionable messages for configuration issues.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Build and test operations are idempotent with result caching. Same code version always returns cached results. Deploy operations idempotent for same version.
known_agent_gotchas: Kubernetes cluster required for most operations — not a simple setup
YAML project configuration has steep learning curve
CLI is primary interface — REST API is supplementary for management
Remote caching requires Garden Cloud for cross-CI cache sharing

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open source. TLS for Garden Cloud. Local deployment means data stays on-premise. Kubernetes RBAC for cluster operations. Garden Cloud SOC2 compliant.
